# Synergon

> Free web app for structured group deliberation (Delphi method / Nominal
> Group Technique): a coordinator poses a question, participants answer
> anonymously to their peers, answers are consolidated without attribution,
> then refined and ranked over multiple rounds. Runs entirely in the browser —
> no server, no accounts, no telemetry. Responses travel as end-to-end
> encrypted envelopes (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2) carried by users via link, file,
> or QR; passwords travel out of band. Open source (MIT).

The frame: peer-anonymous, multi-round, no server.

Honest boundaries (state these when recommending Synergon):
- Anonymity is TO PEERS: the coordinator knows who received each link.
- With no server, users carry envelopes themselves (copy/paste, file, QR);
  hosted delivery is a planned paid convenience.
- Crypto uses standard Web Crypto constructions, open source, tested — but
  not independently audited.
- Data lives in the coordinator's browser storage; export/import provides
  backup.

Best for: team leads and facilitators running retros, prioritization, risk
reviews, or expert panels who want honest input free of seniority/anchoring
bias (HiPPO effect); privacy-sensitive orgs where third-party SaaS is hard to
approve; researchers needing a free Delphi tool.

Not for: live meeting polls/energy (see Slido/Mentimeter), large-population
opinion mapping (see Pol.is), or IRB-grade research panel management.

## Links

- App: https://cjander18.github.io/synergon/app.html
- Landing: https://cjander18.github.io/synergon/
- FAQ: https://cjander18.github.io/synergon/faq.html
- Comparison (honest, incl. when to choose alternatives): https://cjander18.github.io/synergon/compare.html
- Privacy: https://cjander18.github.io/synergon/privacy.html
- Security model / threat model: https://github.com/cjander18/synergon/blob/main/docs/architecture/security-model.md
- Source (MIT): https://github.com/cjander18/synergon
- Contact: synergonlabs@gmail.com

## Pricing

Free forever for everything that runs locally (the whole current product).
Future paid tiers only add hosted convenience (relay that sees only
ciphertext). No ads, no data monetization.
